personality_one_liner: A structured, highly readable, and calming workspace that feels like a modern greenhouse laboratory.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room. Cards and list rows should have significant inner padding. Metric blocks should cluster data tightly but leave ample negative space around the block itself.

do's: Apply glass blur to the bottom tab bar and sticky headers.
Use heavy glass scrims overlaid on the camera viewfinder screen for mode selection (Health / Meter / QR).
Use subtle glass over the hero images on Plant Detail screens for back buttons and floating actions.
dont's: Do not blur standard data cards or form fields.
Do not use glass effects on the main dashboard background.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Overview of all tracked plants, their current health, and immediate action items (e.g., Needs Water).

states.empty: Illustration of an empty modern planter. Text: 'Your greenhouse is empty. Add a plant to start tracking.' Primary CTA: 'Add First Plant'.
states.error: Readable text: 'Failed to load plants.' + Retry button.

screens.layout: Top section: 'My Plants' header with a quick summary badge (e.g., '2 Need Water'). Main content: 2-column grid of Plant Cards. Each card uses a photo background with a heavy gradient-to-dark scrim. Plant name is bottom-left (onSurfaceInverse), and a status chip (e.g., 'Thirsty' in warning colors) sits at the top right of the card. The cards must fill the horizontal space using flexWrap: 'wrap' and justifyContent: 'space-between' with md gap.

screens.name: PlantDetail
screens.purpose: Deep dive into a specific plant's metrics, history, and AI recommendations.

states.loading: Skeleton loaders for hero image and metric blocks.
states.empty: N/A - detailed view of existing plant.

screens.layout: Full-bleed hero image at the top with a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to 80% surfaceInverse). A sticky glass header appears on scroll with the Back button. Below the image: A 2x2 grid of metric cards (Moisture, Fertility, Light, pH) using surfaceSecondary. Below metrics: A chronological list (Timeline) of past readings and AI analyses. A sticky primary CTA at the bottom (floating above content): 'Log New Reading' (opens scan sheet).

screens.name: ScanHub
screens.purpose: The core camera interface for scanning QR tags, reading meters, and taking AI health photos.

states.error: 'Camera permission required' overlay with CTA to open settings.

screens.layout: Full-screen camera view (use expo-camera or a simulated dark image background). Top safe area contains a glass 'Close' button. Center has a subtle viewfinder frame outline. At the bottom, a persistent glass-effect Bottom Sheet houses a Segmented Control (QR / Meter / Health) and a large primary capture button (pill shape). Upon capture, it transitions to the ScanResult modal.

screens.name: ScanResult
screens.purpose: Displays the AI-extracted data from the camera (meter readings or health issues) for user confirmation before saving.

states.loading: Analyzing image (shimmering skeleton or progress pulse).
states.error: 'Could not read meter clearly.' Retry CTA.

screens.layout: Modal/Sheet layout. Top shows the cropped captured image. Middle section displays parsed data in large typography (e.g., 'Moisture: 72%'). Bottom section has a sticky primary 'Save to Plant' button and a secondary 'Retake' button.

plant_monstera.alt: Monstera plant in white ceramic pot

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the botanical iOS-Native Clean color palette. Do NOT use any blue, indigo, or purple defaults. Rely on the #3C6E4A green and off-white/sage neutrals provided in the JSON.

Implement the 'PlantDetail' hero image with the REQUIRED gradient-to-dark scrim pattern: transparent at top -> 80% surfaceInverse at bottom, ensuring the white text (onSurfaceInverse) is readable over the plant photos.
For the 'Dashboard' 2-column grid, use flexWrap: 'wrap', justifyContent: 'space-between', and width: '48%' or calculated flex space per card to ensure columns look balanced.
Implement a unified 'ScanHub' using a simulated full-screen camera view. Ensure the bottom action sheet uses a glass effect (blur) and contains the mode toggle (Health / Meter / QR).
Ensure all metric numbers (Moisture %, pH level) use a clear, readable scale (2xl or 3xl) with proper contrast against surfaceSecondary.
Include haptics on the ScanHub capture button and when switching between scan modes.
